pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) explicitly states 'Create a new style named 'optimized_places''

GeoServer official documentation (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/sld/reference/labeling.html) confirms autoWrap is a valid VendorOption that specifies pixel width for label wrapping. Task description line 19 specifies '80 pixels'.
GeoServer official documentation confirms maxDisplacement is a valid VendorOption for controlling label displacement in pixels. Task description line 20 specifies '50 pixels'.
GeoServer official documentation confirms spaceAround is a valid VendorOption for managing label separation in pixels. Task description line 21 specifies '10 pixels'.
Task description (line 5) explicitly states output path. Confirmed in export_result.sh (line 13). Docker-compose.yml (line 44) confirms /home/ga is a volume mount point on the host system.

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth dataset (public domain geospatial data from naturalearthdata.com)
pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh (lines 85-122) imports Natural Earth shapefiles: ne_110m_admin_0_countries, ne_110m_populated_places, ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines, ne_110m_lakes. Natural Earth is confirmed as a real public domain dataset at https://www.naturalearthdata.com/
Task description states 'ne:ne_populated_places' layer. setup_geoserver.sh (line 120) confirms this layer is imported from Natural Earth data and published to GeoServer.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a SLD style named 'optimized_places' with specific label placement VendorOptions (autoWrap: 80px, maxDisplacement: 50px, spaceAround: 10px) applied to the Natural Earth 1:10m populated places layer in GeoServer 2.25.2. All metadata claims are verified against official GeoServer documentation and task scripts. The data source is the real Natural Earth public domain dataset (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/), not synthetic data. Output must be saved to /home/ga/places_optimized.png.
